title: rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. lanceolatus
intro: Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. lanceolatus, also known as lance-leafed yellow rattle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the broomrape family. This parasitic plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Rhinanthus angustifolius subsp. lanceolatus include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
